---
title: "00 · Introduction"
subtitle: "Tier MML — Mathematics for Machine Learning · 32 lessons walking Deisenroth, Faisal & Ong end-to-end, from linear systems to SVM kernels"
---

## What this tier is for

This tier walks Deisenroth, Faisal & Ong's *Mathematics for Machine
Learning* (Cambridge 2024) lesson by lesson. Where the synthesis
tiers give you the **mathematics** (Tier 5 linear algebra, Tier 5–6
calculus, Tier 5 probability, Tier 6 optimisation), this tier shows
how that mathematics **combines** into the five algorithms that built
classical ML — linear regression, PCA, GMM with EM, SVM with kernels —
and the modern infrastructure they live on: backpropagation, automatic
differentiation, graphical models.

By the end of the tier you can:

- **Solve linear systems** geometrically and algebraically, and recognise
  the design matrix $\boldsymbol{X} \in \mathbb{R}^{N \times D}$ as the
  data structure of ML.
- **Decompose any matrix** — Cholesky, eigendecomposition, SVD — and use
  low-rank approximation (Eckart–Young) for compression and noise removal.
- **Take gradients** of vector- and matrix-valued functions, derive
  **backpropagation** as repeated chain-rule on a computation graph,
  and recognise it as **reverse-mode automatic differentiation**.
- **Reason probabilistically** with the Gaussian, conjugate priors,
  exponential families, and change-of-variables — the four building
  blocks of every Bayesian model.
- **Solve constrained optimisations** via Lagrangians and the KKT
  conditions, and recognise convex problems as the well-behaved
  subfamily.
- **Derive linear regression** as MLE and as orthogonal projection,
  then upgrade it to the Bayesian posterior with closed-form mean and
  covariance.
- **Derive PCA** from four perspectives — max-variance, projection-error,
  eigenvector computation, latent-variable — and recognise probabilistic
  PCA as a Gaussian generative model.
- **Fit Gaussian Mixture Models** with the EM algorithm, then see EM
  itself as coordinate ascent on the ELBO.
- **Derive SVMs** as max-margin separating hyperplanes (primal), then
  as a quadratic program in the dual, and recognise **kernels** as the
  trick that makes the dual lift to infinite-dimensional feature spaces.

## How to read this tier

**Strict prerequisite chain.** Each lesson depends on the previous
one by design. The prerequisite graph in `_curriculum.yml` is the
source of truth — if a lesson links to a synthesis prerequisite
(e.g. `t5.matrices` for the matrix-algebra lesson, `t6.gradient-methods`
for backpropagation), that external prereq is also required.

**Read alongside the MML PDF.** [Free PDF](https://mml-book.com) of
the entire book at `mml-book.com`. This tier adds the *Touch* widget,
the *Wonder* questions, the graduated hints, the glossary cross-links,
and the ML-flavoured framing the textbook's graduate compactness
leaves to the reader.

**Compare with the synthesis tiers** when the same idea is treated
there from a different angle:

- [`t5.matrices`](../tier-5-undergrad-core/01-matrices/index.qmd) /
  [`t5.eigenvalues`](../tier-5-undergrad-core/04-eigenvalues/index.qmd) —
  the pure linear-algebra view, framed without ML.
- [`t6.gradient-methods`](../tier-6-undergrad-advanced/10-gradient-methods/index.qmd) /
  [`t6.convex-optimization`](../tier-6-undergrad-advanced/03-convex-optimization/index.qmd) —
  the pure optimisation view.
- [`t6.spectral-theorem`](../tier-6-undergrad-advanced/04-spectral-theorem/index.qmd) —
  the pure spectral view of SVD.

This tier doesn't re-derive those — it cites them, then applies them
to the ML algorithms the synthesis tiers never reach.

## The lessons by part

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Part I: Foundations<br/>L01-L21<br/>LA, calculus, probability, optimisation]
    B[Part II: Algorithms<br/>L22-L32<br/>ERM, LR, PCA, GMM, SVM]
    A --> B
```

### Part I — Mathematical Foundations (lessons 1-21)

#### Chapter 2 — Linear Algebra

1. [**01 · Systems of Linear Equations and the Geometry of Solutions**](01-linear-systems/index.qmd)
2. [**02 · Matrices: Algebra, Inverse, and the Design Matrix**](02-matrices/index.qmd)
3. [**03 · Vector Spaces and Subspaces**](03-vector-spaces/index.qmd)
4. [**04 · Linear Independence and Basis**](04-linear-independence-basis/index.qmd)
5. [**05 · Rank, Linear Mappings, Change of Basis**](05-rank-and-linear-mappings/index.qmd)
6. [**06 · Affine Spaces and Hyperplanes**](06-affine-spaces/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 3 — Analytic Geometry

7. [**07 · Norms, Inner Products, Lengths, Angles**](07-norms-inner-products/index.qmd)
8. [**08 · Orthonormal Basis, Orthogonal Complement, Function Inner Products**](08-orthogonality/index.qmd)
9. [**09 · Orthogonal Projections and Rotations**](09-projections-rotations/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 4 — Matrix Decompositions

10. [**10 · Determinant, Trace, Eigenvalues**](10-determinant-trace-eigen/index.qmd)
11. [**11 · Cholesky Decomposition**](11-cholesky/index.qmd)
12. [**12 · Eigendecomposition and Diagonalisation**](12-eigendecomposition/index.qmd)
13. [**13 · SVD, Matrix Approximation, Matrix Phylogeny**](13-svd-and-low-rank/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 5 — Vector Calculus

14. [**14 · Univariate and Partial Differentiation, Gradients**](14-differentiation-gradients/index.qmd)
15. [**15 · Gradients of Vector- and Matrix-Valued Functions**](15-vector-matrix-gradients/index.qmd)
16. [**16 · Backpropagation, Automatic Differentiation, Multivariate Taylor**](16-backprop-autodiff/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 6 — Probability and Distributions

17. [**17 · Probability Spaces, Discrete and Continuous, Bayes' Theorem**](17-probability-spaces-bayes/index.qmd)
18. [**18 · Summary Statistics, Independence, the Gaussian**](18-gaussian-summary-stats/index.qmd)
19. [**19 · Conjugacy, the Exponential Family, Change of Variables**](19-conjugacy-change-of-variables/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 7 — Continuous Optimisation

20. [**20 · Gradient Descent, SGD, Momentum**](20-gradient-descent/index.qmd)
21. [**21 · Constrained and Convex Optimisation (Lagrange, KKT)**](21-constrained-convex-optimisation/index.qmd)

### Part II — Central Machine-Learning Problems (lessons 22-32)

#### Chapter 8 — When Models Meet Data

22. [**22 · Data, Models, Empirical Risk Minimisation**](22-models-and-erm/index.qmd)
23. [**23 · Directed Graphical Models and Model Selection**](23-graphical-models-model-selection/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 9 — Linear Regression

24. [**24 · Linear Regression: MLE Parameter Estimation**](24-linear-regression-mle/index.qmd)
25. [**25 · Bayesian Linear Regression and MLE as Orthogonal Projection**](25-bayesian-linear-regression/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 10 — Dimensionality Reduction with PCA

26. [**26 · PCA: Max-Variance and Projection-Error Perspectives**](26-pca-variance-projection/index.qmd)
27. [**27 · PCA: Eigenvector Computation and the High-Dimensional Trick**](27-pca-eigenvector-high-dim/index.qmd)
28. [**28 · PCA as a Latent-Variable Model (Probabilistic PCA)**](28-pca-latent-variable/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 11 — Density Estimation with Gaussian Mixture Models

29. [**29 · Gaussian Mixture Models and MLE**](29-gmm/index.qmd)
30. [**30 · The EM Algorithm and the Latent-Variable Perspective**](30-em-algorithm/index.qmd)

#### Chapter 12 — Classification with Support Vector Machines

31. [**31 · SVM: Separating Hyperplanes and the Primal**](31-svm-primal/index.qmd)
32. [**32 · Dual SVM, Kernels, and Numerical Solution**](32-svm-dual-kernels/index.qmd)

## Track status — complete (32 lessons authored)

Every chapter of Deisenroth, Faisal & Ong is authored against the
six-beat contract. Each lesson carries all required callouts
(fun-fact, key-insight, common-confusion, Takeaways with inline
glossary cross-link, Self-Assessment), graduated hints
(Hint 1 / 2 / 3 / Full solution) on every exercise, and a
sister-synthesis link to the relevant Tier 5–7 lesson.

::: {.callout-note}
This page reflects the snapshot of `_curriculum.yml` at the time of
build. Lesson statuses there are the source of truth — `planned`
lessons are skeletons; `done` lessons are fully authored.
:::
